claims: RETA-LLM reduces hallucination in LLMs by grounding generation in retrieved external knowledge.
RETA-LLM enables answering in-domain questions inaccessible to vanilla LLMs trained on public data.
The disentangled design separates IR systems and LLMs, enabling independent customization of search engines and language models.
LLMs are used not only for answer generation but also for request rewriting, passage extraction, and fact checking.
RETA-LLM provides a complete, ready-to-use pipeline for building domain-specific retrieval-augmented LLM systems from scratch.
RETA-LLM is part of YuLan, an open-source LLM initiative by Gaoling School of Artificial Intelligence, Renmin University of China.
limitations: RETA-LLM is still under development with unresolved issues.
Input length constraints of LLMs necessitate heuristic passage extraction (e.g., sliding window), risking information loss.
Reliance on LLMs for fact checking inherits their inherent unreliability — no external verification oracle is employed.
Default configuration uses a dense retriever without benchmarking against sparse or hybrid alternatives.
No evaluation metrics (e.g., accuracy, faithfulness, retrieval recall) are reported in the provided text.
The toolkit’s effectiveness is not empirically validated in the paper — no experiments, baselines, or ablation studies are presented.

claims: AeroGen is the first model to simultaneously support horizontal and rotated bounding box condition generation for remote sensing image object detection.
The synthetic data produced by AeroGen improve mAP on DIOR, DIOR-R, and HRSC by 3.7%, 4.3%, and 2.43%, respectively.
Rare object classes (GF, DAM, APO) show mAP improvements of 17.8%, 14.7%, and 12.6%, respectively.
The proposed end-to-end data augmentation framework synthesizes RSIOD data directly via conditional generative models, eliminating the need for additional instance-pasting procedures.
The framework integrates a diversity-conditioned generator and a filtering mechanism to enhance both diversity and quality of generated images.
limitations: Dependence on high-quality labeled layout priors (i.e., horizontal and rotated bounding box annotations) for conditional generation.
No evaluation of annotation cost reduction, real-world deployment feasibility, or human-in-the-loop validation.
Limited discussion of generalization to unseen object categories, cross-sensor modalities (e.g., SAR), or domain shifts beyond optical remote sensing.
Computational cost, inference latency, or memory footprint of AeroGen is not reported or benchmarked against alternatives (e.g., GANs or autoregressive models).

claims: LLMs can significantly augment or substitute real-world datasets
Synthetic data boosts model performance in low-resource settings
Reduces annotation costs and enables data augmentation
Improvements of 3-26% observed with synthetic augmentation
